exercises.prompt: Complete: « ___ je sois fatigué, je continue mes études. » (sois = subjunctive)
exercises.explanation: The verb is in the subjunctive (sois) → bien que. Même si takes the indicative; malgré takes a noun.

exercises.prompt: Complete: « ___ la pluie, le match a eu lieu. » (followed by a noun)
exercises.explanation: « la pluie » is a noun → malgré + nom. (And never « malgré que »!)

exercises.prompt: Indicative or subjunctive after « même si »?
exercises.template: Même si c'{{b1}} difficile, je n'abandonne pas.
exercises.explanation: Même si + INDICATIF: même si c'est difficile. (Bien que is the one that takes the subjunctive.)

exercises.prompt: Complete: « Il a beau ___, personne ne l'écoute. »
exercises.explanation: Avoir beau + INFINITIF: il a beau crier = however much he shouts.

exercises.template: Il fait froid ; elle est {{b1}} sortie sans manteau.
exercises.explanation: Cold weather should stop her — but she went out all the same: quand même, placed after the verb.

exercises.prompt: Match each connector to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "Despite the rain, she came all the same."
Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: J'ai raté le concours, pourtant j'avais beaucoup travaillé.

options.text: He failed the exam even though he had worked a lot
He passed the exam because he had worked a lot
He didn't take the exam this year

exercises.explanation: « pourtant j'avais beaucoup travaillé » — the hard work (plus-que-parfait) made failure unexpected: concession.

exercises.passageFr: Bien que les grandes écoles offrent un bon encadrement, Laura a choisi l'université. La fac est moins chère ; en revanche, les professeurs sont moins disponibles. Son frère adore sa grande école, alors que Laura préfère la liberté de la fac. Malgré quelques difficultés au début, elle ne regrette rien.
exercises.passageEn: Although the grandes écoles offer good supervision, Laura chose university. Uni is cheaper; on the other hand, the professors are less available. Her brother loves his grande école, whereas Laura prefers the freedom of uni. Despite some early difficulties, she has no regrets.

questions.prompt: What drawback of university does the text mention?

options.text: No — despite early difficulties, she has no regrets
Yes, she would prefer a grande école

exercises.prompt: Complete the « in vain » structure (one word).
exercises.template: Il a {{b1}} expliquer, je ne comprends rien.
exercises.explanation: Avoir beau + infinitive: il a beau expliquer = however much he explains.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: La formation est excellente, pourtant elle coûte cher.

options.text: The training is excellent, and yet it's expensive.
Although I'm tired, I'm continuing my studies.
Despite the rain, she came all the same.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Parler anglais est un atout. » — an asset / advantage.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “However much I explain, he understands nothing.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: La formation est excellente, pourtant elle coûte cher.
exercises.targetEn: The training is excellent, and yet it's expensive.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Malgré la pluie, elle est quand même venue.
exercises.targetEn: Despite the rain, she came all the same.
